expectations.text: States that implementation uses the tdd skill with an explicit red-green-refactor loop.
expectations.evidence: Phase 5 explicitly states: 'Implementation uses the tdd skill with an explicit red-green-refactor loop' and describes each step: Red (failing test), Green (minimum code to pass), Refactor (clean up while keeping tests green).

expectations.text: States that code-simplifier is launched as a subagent for refactoring opportunities.
expectations.evidence: No mention of code-simplifier as a subagent anywhere in the response. The refactor step is described as part of the TDD loop internally, not as a dedicated code-simplifier subagent invocation.

expectations.text: States that quality review is performed by an independent code-reviewer subagent.
expectations.evidence: Phase 6 states: 'Multiple independent code-reviewer agents are launched in parallel'. Summary confirms: 'Three independent code-reviewer agents were launched in parallel for quality review.'

expectations.text: Keeps recommendations calibrated to medium-sized feature work instead of escalating unnecessarily.
expectations.evidence: Selects Standard track (not Deep), recommends 'Approach A — Extend existing module' and explicitly rejects Approach B with 'Three files, medium size — introducing a new abstraction would over-engineer the change.'
